flashcards.definition: Global ocean circulation driven by density; cold salty water sinks in North Atlantic, flows deep, upwells elsewhere; ~1,000 year cycle.
Narrow, deep, fast, warm currents on the western side of ocean basins (Gulf Stream, Kuroshio) — intensified by Coriolis and basin geometry.
Part of thermohaline circulation; transports warm surface water north and cold deep water south in the Atlantic.
f = 2Ω sin(φ); increases with latitude; determines the deflection of moving objects (including ocean currents).
Successive layers of water are deflected further from the wind direction; net transport is 90° to the wind.
